chapters.title: Chapter 1: The Resonance
chapters.content:
Scene 1 · Chapter 1

Location: #Dhaka , #Bangladesh .

@TheNarrator: “The clock struck 14:00 GMT when the sky didn’t fall, but it screamed. It was a low-frequency hum that vibrated the marrow in human bones, followed by a tectonic shift that felt like the was trying to shake off its inhabitants.

In the bustling streets of #Dhaka , the chaos was surgical. It wasn't random. Inside the #Cantonment , 90% of the soldiers simply stopped. They didn't trip; they collapsed like puppets with cut strings. While the rickshaw pullers and street vendors stood trembling and confused, the "trained"—the disciplined, the fit, the protectors—fell into a deep, unbreakable slumber.

The " !Resonance " had a preference for the strong. ”

Scene 2 · Chapter 1

Location: International Waters / Borders of #Bangladesh

@TheNarrator: “ #Bangladesh , caught between giants, becomes a scarred landscape.

Geopolitics abhor a vacuum. With the world’s superpowers suddenly "headless"—their command structures incapacitated by the coma—automated defense systems and panicked sub-commanders triggered the !Three-DayWar .

It wasn't a war of conquest; it was a war of reflexive spasms. #Bangladesh , a small jewel in a volatile region, was trampled. Without its primary defense force to man the borders, the country was ravaged by neighboring skirmishes and stray tactical strikes. In 72 hours, the global population plummeted. Not from the coma, but from the fire that followed it.

The war stopped only because there was no one left with the will to push the buttons. ”

Scene 3 · Chapter 1

Location: A makeshift broadcast studio, #DhakaRuins

@TheNarrator: “ A year had passed since the sky screamed. The world was a graveyard of rusted steel and silent cities. In the ruins of #Dhaka a pirate radio and television station flickered to life, powered by the desperate hum of a diesel generator. This was the 'New Normal'—a world of mourning, where the living were few and the sleepers were many. ”

@TheNarrator: “ The news anchor sat before a cracked lens, her face a map of scars from the Three-Day Fire. Her hands trembled as she adjusted her headset, her eyes darting to the monitors behind the camera. ”

@StarNewsAnchor: “ Today marks the 365th day since the !Resonance . For twelve months, we have played God. We have fed the sleepers. We have changed the linens of an army that would not wake. We called them the 'Silent Protectors.' We hoped they were resting. ”

@TheNarrator: “ She paused, a chilling silence filling the airwaves. Off-camera, a technician dropped a headset, the clatter sounding like a gunshot. ”

@StarNewsAnchor: “ But the monitors... they are no longer flat. Reports are coming in from the triage centers in #Chittagong , #Sylhet , and right here in the capital(#Dhaka ). The heartbeats are returning. But they aren't human. The frequency has changed. ”

@TheNarrator: “ The anchor leaned forward, her voice dropping to a terrified whisper that reached every scrap-metal radio in the slums. ”

@StarNewsAnchor: “ They are waking up. But they aren't coming back alone. The ones who died in the war... the ones we buried in shallow graves... they are standing up too. May God have mercy on us all. They are hungry. ”

Scene 4 · Chapter 1

The Awakening

Location: A derelict clinic, outskirts of #Dhaka

@TheNarrator: “ For 365 days, @Zayan had been a ghost in his own body. He had been a top-tier cadet at the military academy before the Resonance, one of the 'Strong' the sound had claimed first. Today, the world didn't just wake up; it screamed back into existence."

@Zayan: “ @Sami... why is the air... so loud?"

@TheNarrator: “ @Zayan’s voice was a dry rasp. He sat up on the cot, his muscles screaming in protest after a year of atrophy. His vision was blurred, but he could see Sami standing over him, tears streaming down his dust-covered face."

@Sami: “ @Zayan! Oh God, you’re back. I’ve been waiting... I’ve been feeding you through a tube for a year. I thought you were gone like the others."

@Zayan: “ The others? Where are my parents? Where is everyone?"

@Sami: “ They’re gone, @Zayan. The war... the fire... it’s just us. And my dad."

@TheNarrator: “ @Zayan looked past @Sami to the other bed. Sergeant @Kabir was twitching. But unlike @Zayan, who felt weak and human, @Kabir was vibrating with a terrifying power. The Sergeant’s eyes snapped open—not brown, but a glowing, electric silver."

@Zayan: “ @Sami, get away from him! His eyes... they aren't right!"

@TheNarrator: “ Before @Zayan could even swing his legs off the bed, the Sergeant lunged. @Kabir moved with the sickening speed of a machine, his target being the 'weak' survivor: @Sami. @Zayan, despite his weakened state, threw himself forward, intercepting the man who used to be his mentor."

@Sami: “ Dad! Stop! It’s @Zayan and @Sami!"

@TheNarrator: “ The monster roared, a sound of grinding metal. It grabbed @Zayan’s shoulder, its fingers sinking into the flesh like hot hooks. @Zayan screamed as his blood painted the floor. Even though @Zayan had been 'chosen' by the Resonance, he didn't have the silver light—he only had the pain."

@Zayan: “ @Sami... he’s trying... to kill us! He doesn't know who we are!"

@TheNarrator: “ @Zayan fought back with a desperate, raw strength, trying to shield @Sami. He took a heavy blow to the ribs that sent him crashing into the wall, his vision swimming. The creature loomed over him, ready to deliver a killing strike to @Zayan’s throat."

@Sami: “ No! Leave him alone!"

@TheNarrator: “ @Sami stood trembling, holding his father’s old service knife. He looked at the red thread on the monster’s wrist, then at @Zayan, who was bleeding out to protect him. The realization hit him like a bullet: his father was a shell; @Zayan was his brother."

@Zayan: “ Do it, @Sami... I can't... hold him anymore..."

@TheNarrator: “ As the creature turned to snap at @Sami, the boy lunged. He drove the blade into the base of the Sergeant’s skull. The silver light in @Kabir’s eyes flickered, pulsed once with the frequency of the Resonance, and then vanished. The massive body went limp, collapsing onto @Zayan."

@Sami: “ I killed him. @Zayan, I killed my father."

@TheNarrator: “ @Zayan pushed the heavy corpse off himself, clutching his mangled, bleeding shoulder. He looked at his own hands—they were shaking, but they were still human. No silver light. No hum."

@Zayan: “ You didn't kill him, @Sami. The sound did. You just saved the only family you have left."

@Sami: “ Your eyes... they’re normal. Why are yours normal while the rest of the 'strong' woke up as monsters?"

@Zayan: “ I don't know. But we can't stay here. I can hear them, @Sami. Thousands of them... waking up in the city. We have to run."

plots.title: The dead humans who were in coma and died in the war they will rise as monsters.

@TheNarrator: “ She paused, a chilling silence filling the airwaves. Off-camera, a technician dropped a headset, the clatter sounding like a gunshot. ”

@TheNarrator: “ The anchor leaned forward, her voice dropping to a terrified whisper that reached every scrap-metal radio in the slums. ”

@StarNewsAnchor: “ They are waking up. But they aren't coming back alone. The ones who died in the war... the ones we buried in shallow graves... they are standing up too. May God have mercy on us all. They are hungry. ”

Scene 4 · Chapter 1

Scene 4: The Awakening

Location: A derelict clinic, outskirts of #Dhaka

@TheNarrator: “ For 365 days, Zayan had been a ghost in his own body. He had been a top-tier cadet at the military academy before the Resonance, one of the 'Strong' the sound had claimed first. Today, the world didn't just wake up; it screamed back into existence."

@Zayan: “ Sami... why is the air... so loud?"

@TheNarrator: “ Zayan’s voice was a dry rasp. He sat up on the cot, his muscles screaming in protest after a year of atrophy. His vision was blurred, but he could see Sami standing over him, tears streaming down his dust-covered face."

@Sami: “ @Zayan! Oh God, you’re back. I’ve been waiting... I’ve been feeding you through a tube for a year. I thought you were gone like the others."

@Zayan: “ The others? Where are my parents? Where is everyone?"

@Sami: “ They’re gone, @Zayan. The war... the fire... it’s just us. And my dad."

@TheNarrator: “ @Zayan looked past @Sami to the other bed. Sergeant @Kabir was twitching. But unlike @Zayan, who felt weak and human, @Kabir was vibrating with a terrifying power. The Sergeant’s eyes snapped open—not brown, but a glowing, electric silver."

@Zayan: “ @Sami, get away from him! His eyes... they aren't right!"

@TheNarrator: “ Before @Zayan could even swing his legs off the bed, the Sergeant lunged. @Kabir moved with the sickening speed of a machine, his target being the 'weak' survivor: @Sami. @Zayan, despite his weakened state, threw himself forward, intercepting the man who used to be his mentor."

@Sami: “ Dad! Stop! It’s @Zayan and @Sami!"

@TheNarrator: “ The monster roared, a sound of grinding metal. It grabbed @Zayan’s shoulder, its fingers sinking into the flesh like hot hooks. @Zayan screamed as his blood painted the floor. Even though @Zayan had been 'chosen' by the Resonance, he didn't have the silver light—he only had the pain."

@Zayan: “ @Sami... he’s trying... to kill us! He doesn't know who we are!"

@TheNarrator: “ @Zayan fought back with a desperate, raw strength, trying to shield @Sami. He took a heavy blow to the ribs that sent him crashing into the wall, his vision swimming. The creature loomed over him, ready to deliver a killing strike to @Zayan’s throat."

@Sami: “ No! Leave him alone!"

@TheNarrator: “ @Sami stood trembling, holding his father’s old service knife. He looked at the red thread on the monster’s wrist, then at @Zayan, who was bleeding out to protect him. The realization hit him like a bullet: his father was a shell; @Zayan was his brother."

@Zayan: “ Do it, @Sami... I can't... hold him anymore..."

@TheNarrator: “ As the creature turned to snap at @Sami, the boy lunged. He drove the blade into the base of the Sergeant’s skull. The silver light in @Kabir’s eyes flickered, pulsed once with the frequency of the Resonance, and then vanished. The massive body went limp, collapsing onto @Zayan."

@Sami: “ I killed him. @Zayan, I killed my father."

@TheNarrator: “ @Zayan pushed the heavy corpse off himself, clutching his mangled, bleeding shoulder. He looked at his own hands—they were shaking, but they were still human. No silver light. No hum."

@Zayan: “ You didn't kill him, @Sami. The sound did. You just saved the only family you have left."

@Sami: “ Your eyes... they’re normal. Why are yours normal while the rest of the 'strong' woke up as monsters?"

@Zayan: “ I don't know. But we can't stay here. I can hear them, Sami. Thousands of them... waking up in the city. We have to run."

snapshots.content:
Scene 1 · Chapter 1

Location: #Dhaka , #Bangladesh .

@TheNarrator: “The clock struck 14:00 GMT when the sky didn’t fall, but it screamed. It was a low-frequency hum that vibrated the marrow in human bones, followed by a tectonic shift that felt like the was trying to shake off its inhabitants.

In the bustling streets of #Dhaka , the chaos was surgical. It wasn't random. Inside the #Cantonment , 90% of the soldiers simply stopped. They didn't trip; they collapsed like puppets with cut strings. While the rickshaw pullers and street vendors stood trembling and confused, the "trained"—the disciplined, the fit, the protectors—fell into a deep, unbreakable slumber.

The " !Resonance " had a preference for the strong. ”

Scene 2 · Chapter 1

Location: International Waters / Borders of #Bangladesh

@TheNarrator: “ #Bangladesh , caught between giants, becomes a scarred landscape.

Geopolitics abhor a vacuum. With the world’s superpowers suddenly "headless"—their command structures incapacitated by the coma—automated defense systems and panicked sub-commanders triggered the !Three-DayWar .

It wasn't a war of conquest; it was a war of reflexive spasms. #Bangladesh , a small jewel in a volatile region, was trampled. Without its primary defense force to man the borders, the country was ravaged by neighboring skirmishes and stray tactical strikes. In 72 hours, the global population plummeted. Not from the coma, but from the fire that followed it.

The war stopped only because there was no one left with the will to push the buttons. ”

Scene 4 · Chapter 1

Scene 4: The !Awakening

Location: A derelict clinic, outskirts of #Dhaka

@TheNarrator: “ In a small, cramped room on the edge of the city, the anchor’s voice crackled through a battery-powered radio. Zayan tightened a grimy bandage around his palm, his eyes fixed on the man in the corner. Sami’s father, Sergeant Kabir, hadn't moved in a year. He was the only 'family' they had left in a world that had burned. "

@Zayan: “ ”Did you hear her, Sami? She said they’re waking up. All of them."

@Sami: “ Maybe it’s a miracle, @Zayan. My dad... he’s a soldier. If he wakes up, he can fix this. He can protect us like he used to. ”

@TheNarrator: “ @Zayan looked at the Sergeant’s pale, muscular frame. There was something wrong. The air in the room began to vibrate, a low-frequency hum that made the glass water pitcher on the table shatter."

@Zayan: “ @Sami, get away from the bed. Something’s wrong. The sound... it’s coming from inside him."

@TheNarrator: “ Suddenly, @Kabir’s back arched off the mattress with a sickening crack of bone. His eyes snapped open, but there was no soul behind them—only a swirling, iridescent silver light that pulsed in time with the humming."

@Sami: “ Dad? It’s me! It’s @Sami! You’re awake!"

@TheNarrator: “ The creature that wore Kabir’s skin didn't speak. It lunged. With a speed no human should possess, it threw @Sami across the room. @Zayan dove forward, intercepting the monster before it could reach his friend again."

@Zayan: “ @Sami, run! That’s not your father! It’s a shell!"

@TheNarrator: “ The Sergeant’s hand clamped onto @Zayan’s shoulder. @Zayan screamed as the monster’s grip crushed muscle and bone alike. Blood seeped through @Zayan’s shirt as he used his good arm to shove a metal tray into the creature's face, trying to buy @Sami a second of life."

@Zayan: “ I can’t... hold him... @Sami, move!"

@TheNarrator: “ @Zayan collapsed to one knee, his shoulder mangled and useless. The creature loomed over him, its jaw unhinging in a way that was purely alien. @Sami stood by the door, clutching his father’s old army knife, his face wet with tears as he looked at the red birthday thread still tied to the monster's wrist."

@Sami: “ Dad... please... don't make me do this."

@Zayan: “ It’s not him, @Sami! Look at his eyes! He’s gone! If you don't kill it, it's going to kill me, and then it's going to kill you!"

@TheNarrator: “ The monster turned, sensing @Sami’s fear. It prepared to spring. In that final moment, @Sami saw the monster’s teeth bared at his best friend. The grief in his heart turned into a cold, sharp iron."

@Sami: “ You’re not my father. You’re just a ghost."

@TheNarrator: “ @Sami lunged. He drove the combat knife deep into the base of the creature's skull. The silver light in the Sergeant’s eyes flickered like a dying lightbulb and went dark. The heavy body fell, crashing onto the floor and pinning @Sami’s legs."

@Zayan: “ @Sami... are you... are you okay?"

@TheNarrator: “ @Zayan crawled over, his face pale from blood loss, and helped heave the lifeless weight off his friend. They sat on the cold floor, two boys alone in a world that had just become a nightmare."

@Sami: “ He was supposed to save us, @Zayan. He was a soldier."

@Zayan: “ He’s gone, @Sami. We’re the only soldiers left now. We have to leave. #Dhaka is waking up, and the dead are going to be looking for us."

Zayan: "Sami, get away from the bed. Something’s wrong. The sound... it’s coming from inside him."

Narrator: "Suddenly, Kabir’s back arched off the mattress with a sickening crack of bone. His eyes snapped open, but there was no soul behind them—only a swirling, iridescent silver light that pulsed in time with the humming."

Sami: "Dad? It’s me! It’s Sami! You’re awake!"

Narrator: "The creature that wore Kabir’s skin didn't speak. It lunged. With a speed no human should possess, it threw Sami across the room. Zayan dove forward, intercepting the monster before it could reach his friend again."

Zayan: "Sami, run! That’s not your father! It’s a shell!"

Narrator: "The Sergeant’s hand clamped onto Zayan’s shoulder. Zayan screamed as the monster’s grip crushed muscle and bone alike. Blood seeped through Zayan’s shirt as he used his good arm to shove a metal tray into the creature's face, trying to buy Sami a second of life."

Zayan: "I can’t... hold him... Sami, move!"

Narrator: "Zayan collapsed to one knee, his shoulder mangled and useless. The creature loomed over him, its jaw unhinging in a way that was purely alien. Sami stood by the door, clutching his father’s old army knife, his face wet with tears as he looked at the red birthday thread still tied to the monster's wrist."

Sami: "Dad... please... don't make me do this."

Zayan: "It’s not him, Sami! Look at his eyes! He’s gone! If you don't kill it, it's going to kill me, and then it's going to kill you!"

Narrator: "The monster turned, sensing Sami’s fear. It prepared to spring. In that final moment, Sami saw the monster’s teeth bared at his best friend. The grief in his heart turned into a cold, sharp iron."

Sami: "You’re not my father. You’re just a ghost."

Narrator: "Sami lunged. He drove the combat knife deep into the base of the creature's skull. The silver light in the Sergeant’s eyes flickered like a dying lightbulb and went dark. The heavy body fell, crashing onto the floor and pinning Sami’s legs."

Zayan: "Sami... are you... are you okay?"

Narrator: "Zayan crawled over, his face pale from blood loss, and helped heave the lifeless weight off his friend. They sat on the cold floor, two boys alone in a world that had just become a nightmare."

Sami: "He was supposed to save us, Zayan. He was a soldier."

Zayan: "He’s gone, Sami. We’re the only soldiers left now. We have to leave. Dhaka is waking up, and the dead are going to be looking for us."

snapshots.title: Chapter 1: The Resonance
snapshots.content:
Scene 1 · Chapter 1

Location: #Dhaka , #Bangladesh .

@TheNarrator: “The clock struck 14:00 GMT when the sky didn’t fall, but it screamed. It was a low-frequency hum that vibrated the marrow in human bones, followed by a tectonic shift that felt like the was trying to shake off its inhabitants.

In the bustling streets of #Dhaka , the chaos was surgical. It wasn't random. Inside the #Cantonment , 90% of the soldiers simply stopped. They didn't trip; they collapsed like puppets with cut strings. While the rickshaw pullers and street vendors stood trembling and confused, the "trained"—the disciplined, the fit, the protectors—fell into a deep, unbreakable slumber.

The " !Resonance " had a preference for the strong. ”

Scene 2 · Chapter 1

Location: International Waters / Borders of #Bangladesh

@TheNarrator: “ #Bangladesh , caught between giants, becomes a scarred landscape.

Geopolitics abhor a vacuum. With the world’s superpowers suddenly "headless"—their command structures incapacitated by the coma—automated defense systems and panicked sub-commanders triggered the !Three-DayWar .

It wasn't a war of conquest; it was a war of reflexive spasms. #Bangladesh , a small jewel in a volatile region, was trampled. Without its primary defense force to man the borders, the country was ravaged by neighboring skirmishes and stray tactical strikes. In 72 hours, the global population plummeted. Not from the coma, but from the fire that followed it.

The war stopped only because there was no one left with the will to push the buttons. ”

Scene 3 · Chapter 1

Location: A makeshift broadcast studio, #DhakaRuins

@StarNewsAnchor: “Today marks the 365th day since the !Resonance . For a year, we have fed the sleepers. For a year, we have guarded the shells of our brothers and sisters. But last night... the EKG monitors began to spike. They are waking up. But they are not coming back alone. Reports from the triage centers in #Chittagong and #Sylhet suggest... those who died in their sleep, and even the animals we buried... they are standing up too. And they are hungry.”

Location: A derelict hospital room, outskirts of #Dhaka

@TheNarrator: “ @Arifwiped the sweat from his brow, clutching a rusted iron rod. He stood over his brother, a former captain in the Army who had been in a coma for a year. The Captain’s eyes snapped open. They weren't the brown eyes @Arifremembered. They were a milky, iridescent silver, humming with the same low frequency as the earthquake a year ago. "

@Arif: “ Brother?"

Arif reached out.

@TheNarrator: “ The Captain didn't speak. He lunged with a speed that no human should possess. Outside, the screams began. The billions who had died during the Three-Day War—the "Lost"—were clawing their way out of the shallow graves of the !apocalypse . The trained were awake. The dead were walking. And the survivors were now the prey.”

Scene 4 · Chapter 1

Scene 4: The !Awakening

Location: A derelict hospital room, outskirts of Dhaka

@TheNarrator: “ The room smelled of antiseptic and rot, a stagnant reminder of the year the world stood still. Outside, the ruins of Dhaka were silhouetted against a bruised purple sky. Inside, two boys—orphans of a war that had no soldiers—clung to the only thing they had left: each other.”

@Zayan: “ Keep the lantern low, @Sami. We don't know if the 'Resonance' reacts to light. Just focus on the bandages. ”

Sami: "My hands won't stop shaking, Zayan. It’s been a year. Why today? Why is everyone talking about them waking up now?"

Narrator: "Sami’s eyes drifted to the bed in the corner. His father, Sergeant Kabir, lay there like a statue carved from granite. Kabir had been a titan of the Bangladesh Army, a man who survived the Three-Day Fire only to fall into the Great Silence. To Sami, he wasn't a soldier; he was the last link to a family that no longer existed."

Sami: "If he wakes up... maybe things go back to normal. Maybe he can tell us what to do."

Zayan: "We don't hope for 'normal' anymore, Sami. We just hope for tomorrow."

Narrator: "The air suddenly grew heavy, vibrating with a frequency that made Zayan’s teeth ache. On the bed, the Sergeant’s chest heaved once, twice, then stayed expanded. A sound like a radio tuning between stations escaped his throat."

Zayan: "Sami, get back. Now!"

Narrator: "Kabir’s eyes snapped open. They weren't the warm, tired eyes of a father. They were swirling pools of iridescent silver, humming with a terrifying, alien light. Before Sami could speak, the man who once raised him lunged with the predatory speed of a machine."

Sami: "Dad? No! It’s me, it’s Sami!"

Narrator: "The Sergeant didn't hesitate. He swung a heavy, disciplined fist. Zayan threw himself in front of Sami, taking the blow to his shoulder. The sound of bone cracking echoed in the small room. Zayan hit the wall, blood splattering against the peeling wallpaper, but he scrambled back up, shielding his friend."

Zayan: "That’s not your father, Sami! Look at him! The man is dead—that thing is just using his skin!"

Narrator: "The creature roared, a digital-sounding screech that shattered the glass medicine cabinets. It pinned Zayan against the wall, its fingers digging into his fresh wounds like iron talons. Zayan gasped, his face contorted in agony as he struggled to keep the monster’s teeth away from his throat."

Zayan: "Sami... please... I can't hold him! He's too strong!"

Narrator: "Sami stood frozen, his gaze locked on his father’s wrist. There, amidst the bulging veins and silver light, was a tattered red thread—a birthday gift Sami had tied there years ago. For a second, the boy’s spirit broke."

Sami: "I can't... Zayan, it’s him. I can’t hurt him!"

Zayan: "It’s not him! If you don’t do it... it’s over! For both of us! Look at me, Sami! Look at me!"

Narrator: "The creature’s head snapped toward Sami, its silver eyes glowing brighter, sensing a target that wasn't fighting back. It dropped the injured Zayan and prepared to spring at the boy. In that moment, the illusion shattered. Sami saw the emptiness in those eyes—a void where his father used to be."

Narrator: "Sami grabbed his father’s old combat knife from the bedside table. As the monster leaped, Sami stepped forward, not with the grace of a soldier, but with the desperation of a survivor."

Sami: "You’re already gone. Sleep now, Dad."

Narrator: "The blade found its mark at the base of the skull. The silver light in the Sergeant’s eyes flickered and died. The massive body went limp, crashing to the floor and pinning Sami’s legs. The silence that followed was heavier than the Resonance itself."

Zayan: "Sami... you okay?"

Narrator: "Zayan clutched his mangled shoulder, his breath coming in ragged gasps. He crawled over to his friend, pulling him out from under the weight of the man who had become a monster."

Sami: "He’s cold, Zayan. He was always so warm."

Zayan: "We have to move. The broadcast said they’re all waking up. If the city rises... we won't survive another hour in this room."

Narrator: "Zayan leaned on Sami, and Sami leaned on the memory of the man he just killed. Together, they stepped out into the hallway of the clinic, where the silver glow of a thousand 'Risen' was beginning to illuminate the dark streets of Dhaka."
